dsl_rule.translation_notes: Interpreted 'timing oscillator with period shorter than 5 days' as Stochastic with 5-day period (stoch_k_5). Overbought/oversold thresholds set at >80 and <20 respectively. Entry window of 3-5 days represented by hold_days_max of 5. Exit conditions based on reversal from extreme levels (approaching midpoint of 50). Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short entries based on oscillator extremes. Target regimes set to TRENDING as specified.

backtest_rule.reason: Interpreted 'timing oscillator with period shorter than 5 days' as Stochastic with 5-day period (stoch_k_5). Overbought/oversold thresholds set at >80 and <20 respectively. Entry window of 3-5 days represented by hold_days_max of 5. Exit conditions based on reversal from extreme levels (approaching midpoint of 50). Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long and short entries based on oscillator extremes. Target regimes set to TRENDING as specified.

source_rule_text: Use a timing oscillator with a period shorter than the trend system's holding period to generate better entry points within the first 10%-20% of the trend trade, with the oscillator reaching overbought/oversold levels approximately every 5 days.
